Dividing both sides by \( |x_2 - x_1| \), we get \( \sqrt{5} = \sqrt{1 + (x_2 + x_1)^2} \), so \( 5 = 1 + (x_2 + x_1)^2 \), so \( (x_2 + x_1)^2 = 4 \), so \( x_2 + x_1 = \pm 2 \). The area of the square is the side length squared, which is \( 5(x_2 - x_1)^2 \). The minimum value of \( (x_2 - x_1)^2 \) occurs when \( x_2 + x_1 = 0 \), so \( x_2 = -x_1 \), and \( (x_2 - x_1)^2 = (2x_1)^2 = 4x_1^2 \).
